Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotTable

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotElement
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotTable
All Implemented Interfaces:
IPotElement
public class PotTable extends PotElement
Represents a table element within a presentation slide.

This class provides a fluent API for creating, formatting, and manipulating tables in PowerPoint slides. It wraps the underlying Apache POI XSLFTable object and offers methods for setting data, adjusting borders, applying fills, formatting text, and managing rows and columns. All operations return the table instance for method chaining.

Basic Usage Example


 PotTable table = slide.addTable(3, 4)
     .at(100, 100)
     .size(600, 200);

 // Set table data
 table.setData(new String[][] {
     {"Name", "Age", "City", "Country"},
     {"Alice", "25", "Beijing", "China"},
     {"Bob", "30", "London", "UK"}
 });

 // Format the header row
 table.row(0).fill(PotColor.BLUE).fontColor(PotColor.WHITE);

Cell Merging Example


 table.mergeCells(0, 0, 0, 3);  // Merge first row across all columns
Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    PotTable.PotTableColumn
    Provides a fluent interface for formatting a single column in a table.
    static class 
    PotTable.PotTableRow
    Provides a fluent interface for formatting a single row in a table.

    Attaches an action (e.g., mouseclick or mouseover) to the element.

    Brings this element to the front of the slide's drawing order.

  • Method Details

    • getRowCount

      public int getRowCount()
      Returns the number of rows in the table.
      Returns:
      the current row count

    • getColumnCount

      public int getColumnCount()
      Returns the number of columns in the table.
      Returns:
      the current column count

    • cell

      public PotTableCell cell(int row, int col)
      Returns a wrapper for the cell at the specified row and column indices.

      The returned PotTableCell object provides methods for formatting and manipulating the individual cell.

      Parameters:
      row - the zero-based row index
      col - the zero-based column index
      Returns:
      a PotTableCell wrapper for the specified cell
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if either index is out of range

    • row

      public PotTable.PotTableRow row(int row)
      Returns a wrapper for the row at the specified index.

      The returned PotTable.PotTableRow object provides methods for applying formatting operations to all cells in the row.

      Parameters:
      row - the zero-based row index
      Returns:
      a PotTableRow wrapper for the specified row
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the row index is out of range

    • column

      public PotTable.PotTableColumn column(int col)
      Returns a wrapper for the column at the specified index.

      The returned PotTable.PotTableColumn object provides methods for applying formatting operations to all cells in the column.

      Parameters:
      col - the zero-based column index
      Returns:
      a PotTableColumn wrapper for the specified column
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the column index is out of range

    • setData

      public PotTable setData(String[][] data)
      Populates the table with data from a two-dimensional string array.

      This method sets the text content of table cells using the provided data array. If the data array has fewer rows or columns than the table, only the overlapping cells are updated. Extra rows or columns in the data array are ignored.

      Parameters:
      data - a two-dimensional string array containing the cell values
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • getData

      public String[][] getData()
      Retrieves all text content from the table as a two-dimensional string array.

      The returned array has dimensions matching the current row and column counts. Empty cells are represented as empty strings or null, depending on the underlying POI implementation.

      Returns:
      a two-dimensional string array containing all cell text values

    • addRow

      public PotTable addRow()
      Appends a new row to the bottom of the table.

      The new row will have the same number of cells as existing rows. All cells in the new row are initially empty.

      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • insertRow

      public PotTable insertRow(int index)
      Inserts a new row at the specified position.

      Existing rows at or after the insertion index are shifted down. The new row will have the same number of cells as existing rows.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based insertion position
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is less than 0 or greater than the row count

    • removeRow

      public PotTable removeRow(int index)
      Removes the row at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the row to remove
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range

    • setRowHeight

      public PotTable setRowHeight(int index, double height)
      Sets the height of a specific row.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based row index
      height - the new height in points
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range
      IllegalArgumentException - if the height is not positive

    • getRowHeight

      public double getRowHeight(int index)
      Returns the height of the row at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based row index
      Returns:
      the row height in points
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range

    • addColumn

      public PotTable addColumn()
      Appends a new column to the right side of the table.

      A new cell is added to every existing row in the table.

      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • insertColumn

      public PotTable insertColumn(int index)
      Inserts a new column at the specified position.

      Existing columns at or after the insertion index are shifted right. A new cell is inserted at the specified position in every row.

      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based insertion position
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is less than 0 or greater than the column count

    • removeColumn

      public PotTable removeColumn(int index)
      Removes the column at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index of the column to remove
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range

    • setColumnWidth

      public PotTable setColumnWidth(int index, double width)
      Sets the width of a specific column.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based column index
      width - the new width in points
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range
      IllegalArgumentException - if the width is not positive

    • getColumnWidth

      public double getColumnWidth(int index)
      Returns the width of the column at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based column index
      Returns:
      the column width in points
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of range

    • mergeCells

      public PotTable mergeCells(int startRow, int startCol, int endRow, int endCol)
      Merges a rectangular range of cells into a single cell.

      The merged cell will span from the start row/column to the end row/column inclusive. The content of the top-left cell in the range is preserved; other cell contents are discarded.

      Parameters:
      startRow - the zero-based starting row index
      startCol - the zero-based starting column index
      endRow - the zero-based ending row index
      endCol - the zero-based ending column index
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if any index is out of range
      IllegalArgumentException - if startRow > endRow or startCol > endCol

    • border

      public PotTable border(PotBorder border)
      Applies the specified border style to all cells in the table.

      This method sets all four borders (top, bottom, left, right) of every cell to the specified border style.

      Parameters:
      border - the border style to apply
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • innerBorder

      public PotTable innerBorder(PotBorder border)
      Applies the specified border style to all interior cell borders.

      This method sets borders between adjacent cells while leaving the outer perimeter of the table unchanged. Specifically, it applies:

      • Top border to all cells except those in the first row
      • Left border to all cells except those in the first column
      • Bottom border to all cells except those in the last row
      • Right border to all cells except those in the last column

      Parameters:
      border - the border style to apply
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • outerBorder

      public PotTable outerBorder(PotBorder border)
      Applies the specified border style to the outer perimeter of the table.

      This method sets borders only on the outermost cells of the table:

      • Top border to all cells in the first row
      • Left border to all cells in the first column
      • Bottom border to all cells in the last row
      • Right border to all cells in the last column

      Parameters:
      border - the border style to apply
      Returns:
      this table for method chaining

    • noBorder

      public PotTable noBorder()
      Removes all borders from every cell in the table.

      This method clears any border style applied to all cells, making the table appear borderless.

      Usage Example

      
 table.noBorder();
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.noBorder()

    • fill

      public PotTable fill(PotColor color)
      Sets the background fill color for all cells in the table.

      This method applies the specified color to the background of every cell, overriding any previous fill settings.

      Usage Example

      
 table.fill(PotColor.LIGHT_GRAY);
      Parameters:
      color - the color to use for the cell background fill
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.fill(PotColor)

    • fillRow

      public PotTable fillRow(int row, PotColor color)
      Sets the background fill color for all cells in a specific row.

      This method applies the specified color to the background of every cell in the given row.

      Usage Example

      
 // Fill the header row with blue
 table.fillRow(0, PotColor.BLUE);
      Parameters:
      row - the zero-based index of the row to fill
      color - the color to use for the cell background fill
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the row index is invalid
      See Also:
      • fillColumn(int, PotColor)
      • fillRange(int, int, int, int, PotColor)

    • fillColumn

      public PotTable fillColumn(int col, PotColor color)
      Sets the background fill color for all cells in a specific column.

      This method applies the specified color to the background of every cell in the given column.

      Usage Example

      
 // Highlight the first column
 table.fillColumn(0, PotColor.YELLOW);
      Parameters:
      col - the zero-based index of the column to fill
      color - the color to use for the cell background fill
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the column index is invalid
      See Also:
      • fillRow(int, PotColor)
      • fillRange(int, int, int, int, PotColor)

    • fillRange

      public PotTable fillRange(int startRow, int startCol, int endRow, int endCol, PotColor color)
      Sets the background fill color for a rectangular range of cells.

      This method applies the specified color to the background of all cells within the inclusive range defined by the start and end row/column indices.

      Usage Example

      
 // Fill a 2x2 block starting at cell (1,1)
 table.fillRange(1, 1, 2, 2, PotColor.GREEN);
      Parameters:
      startRow - the zero-based starting row index (inclusive)
      startCol - the zero-based starting column index (inclusive)
      endRow - the zero-based ending row index (inclusive)
      endCol - the zero-based ending column index (inclusive)
      color - the color to use for the cell background fill
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if any row or column index is invalid
      IllegalArgumentException - if startRow > endRow or startCol > endCol
      See Also:
      • fillRow(int, PotColor)
      • fillColumn(int, PotColor)

    • alternateRowColors

      public PotTable alternateRowColors(PotColor color1, PotColor color2)
      Applies alternating background colors to rows.

      This method sets the background fill for rows in a striped pattern. Even-indexed rows (0, 2, 4, ...) receive color1, and odd-indexed rows receive color2.

      Usage Example

      
 table.alternateRowColors(PotColor.WHITE, PotColor.LIGHT_BLUE);
      Parameters:
      color1 - the color for even-indexed rows (starting from row 0)
      color2 - the color for odd-indexed rows
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • alternateColumnColors(PotColor, PotColor)

    • alternateColumnColors

      public PotTable alternateColumnColors(PotColor color1, PotColor color2)
      Applies alternating background colors to columns.

      This method sets the background fill for columns in a striped pattern. Even-indexed columns (0, 2, 4, ...) receive color1, and odd-indexed columns receive color2.

      Usage Example

      
 table.alternateColumnColors(PotColor.WHITE, PotColor.LIGHT_GRAY);
      Parameters:
      color1 - the color for even-indexed columns (starting from column 0)
      color2 - the color for odd-indexed columns
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • alternateRowColors(PotColor, PotColor)

    • fontFamily

      public PotTable fontFamily(String family)
      Sets the font family for text in all cells.

      This method applies the specified font family to the text content of every cell in the table.

      Usage Example

      
 table.fontFamily("Arial");
      Parameters:
      family - the name of the font family (e.g., "Calibri", "Times New Roman")
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.fontFamily(String)

    • fontSize

      public PotTable fontSize(double size)
      Sets the font size for text in all cells.

      This method applies the specified font size (in points) to the text content of every cell in the table.

      Usage Example

      
 table.fontSize(12.0);
      Parameters:
      size - the font size in points
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if size is not positive
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.fontSize(double)

    • fontColor

      public PotTable fontColor(PotColor color)
      Sets the font color for text in all cells.

      This method applies the specified color to the text content of every cell in the table.

      Usage Example

      
 table.fontColor(PotColor.BLACK);
      Parameters:
      color - the color to use for the text
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.fontColor(PotColor)

    • align

      public PotTable align(PotAlignment alignment)
      Sets the horizontal text alignment for all cells.

      This method applies the specified horizontal alignment to the text content of every cell in the table.

      Usage Example

      
 table.align(PotAlignment.CENTER);
      Parameters:
      alignment - the horizontal alignment to apply
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.align(PotAlignment)

    • verticalAlign

      public PotTable verticalAlign(PotVerticalAlignment alignment)
      Sets the vertical text alignment for all cells.

      This method applies the specified vertical alignment to the text content of every cell in the table.

      Usage Example

      
 table.verticalAlign(PotVerticalAlignment.MIDDLE);
      Parameters:
      alignment - the vertical alignment to apply
      Returns:
      this PotTable instance for method chaining
      See Also:
      • PotTableCell.verticalAlign(PotVerticalAlignment)

    • duplicate

      public PotTable duplicate()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Creates a copy of this element on the same slide.

      Supported element types

      • PotShape geometric shapes
      • PotTextBox text boxes
      • PotImage images
      • PotTable tables
      • PotConnector connector lines

      Other element types such as PotAudio or PotVideo are not supported and will throw a PotException.

      Usage Example

      
 PotTextBox original = slide.addTextBox("Original");
 PotTextBox copy = original.duplicate();
 copy.setText("Copy").move(100, 0);
      Specified by:
      duplicate in interface IPotElement
      Overrides:
      duplicate in class PotElement
      Returns:
      a new element that is a duplicate of this one

    • getRawTable

      public org.apache.poi.xslf.usermodel.XSLFTable getRawTable()
      Returns the underlying Apache POI XSLFTable object.

      This method provides direct access to the internal POI object for advanced operations not covered by the PotTable API. Modifications made directly to the returned object may affect the behavior of this PotTable instance.

      Returns:
      the underlying XSLFTable object.

    • at

      public PotTable at(double x, double y)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the position of the element to the specified coordinates.
      Overrides:
      at in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      x - the new X-coordinate, in points
      y - the new Y-coordinate, in points
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • move

      Parameters:
      width - the new width, in points; must be positive
      height - the new height, in points; must be positive

    • scale

      public PotTable scale(double scale)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Scales the element uniformly by the specified factor.
      Overrides:
      scale in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      scale - the scaling factor; 1.0 represents 100%, must be positive
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • scale

      public PotTable scale(double scaleX, double scaleY)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Scales the element independently along the X and Y axes.
      Overrides:
      scale in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      scaleX - the horizontal scaling factor; must be positive
      scaleY - the vertical scaling factor; must be positive
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotate

      public PotTable rotate(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotation

      public PotTable rotation(double angle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the rotation angle of the element (fluent builder alias).
      Overrides:
      rotation in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      angle - the rotation angle in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • rotateBy

      public PotTable rotateBy(double deltaAngle)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Rotates the element by the specified angle delta.

      Rotation is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      rotateBy in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      deltaAngle - the angle to add to the current rotation, in degrees
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipHorizontal

      public PotTable flipHorizontal()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the horizontal flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      flipHorizontal in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • flipVertical

      public PotTable flipVertical()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Toggles the vertical flip state of the element.

      Flipping is only applied to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      flipVertical in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringToFront

      public PotTable bringToFront()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element to the front of the slide's drawing order.

      This operation manipulates the underlying XML structure of the slide.

      Overrides:
      bringToFront in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendToBack

      public PotTable sendToBack()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element to the back of the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendToBack in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • bringForward

      public PotTable bringForward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Brings this element one step forward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      bringForward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • sendBackward

      public PotTable sendBackward()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sends this element one step backward in the slide's drawing order.

      This method uses internal XML manipulation because Apache POI does not provide a direct API for zorder changes.

      Overrides:
      sendBackward in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • animate

      public PotTable animate(PotAnimation animation)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies an animation to this element.
      Overrides:
      animate in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      animation - the animation to apply; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAnimation

      public PotTable removeAnimation()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any animation attached to this element.
      Overrides:
      removeAnimation in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotTable hyperlink(String url)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink to this element that points to an external URL.

      Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      url - the destination URL; must not be null
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlink

      public PotTable hyperlink(PotHyperlink link)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches a hyperlink defined by a PotHyperlink object.

      If the provided link is null, any existing hyperlink is removed. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlink in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      link - the hyperlink configuration, or null to remove the hyperlink
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • hyperlinkToSlide

      public PotTable hyperlinkToSlide(int slideIndex)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Creates a hyperlink that jumps to a specific slide within the same presentation.

      This method sets both the internal slide reference and a fallback address string. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      hyperlinkToSlide in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      slideIndex - the zerobased index of the target slide
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeHyperlink

      public PotTable removeHyperlink()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any hyperlink attached to this element.

      This method manipulates the underlying XML because Apache POI does not provide a direct removal API. Hyperlinks are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected.

      Overrides:
      removeHyperlink in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • action

      public PotTable action(PotAction action)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Attaches an action (e.g., mouseclick or mouseover) to the element.
      Overrides:
      action in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      action - the action configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeAction

      public PotTable removeAction(boolean isHover)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes an action from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeAction in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      isHover - true to remove a mouseover action, false to remove a mouseclick action
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • shadow

      public PotTable shadow(PotShadow shadow)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a shadow effect to the element.

      Shadows are only applicable to simple shapes; other shape types are unaffected. Passing null or a shadow of type PotShadow.Type.NONE removes any existing shadow.

      Overrides:
      shadow in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      shadow - the shadow configuration, or null to remove the shadow
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • removeShadow

      public PotTable removeShadow()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes any shadow effect from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeShadow in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a reflection effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      reflection in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      reflection - the reflection configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Parameters:

      Returns:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

      public PotTable removeEffects()
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Removes all visual effects (shadow, reflection, glow, softedge, 3D) from the element.
      Overrides:
      removeEffects in class PotElement
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

      Overrides:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

      Parameters:

      Parameters:

      Returns:

    • lighting

      public PotTable lighting(Pot3DFormat.Lighting lighting)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Applies a lighting effect to the element.
      Overrides:
      lighting in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      lighting - the lighting configuration
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • opacity

      public PotTable opacity(double opacity)
      Description copied from class: PotElement
      Sets the opacity (transparency) of the element.

      Opacity is applied by manipulating the underlying XML, as Apache POI does not provide a direct API. The value must be between 0.0 (fully transparent) and 1.0 (fully opaque).

      Overrides:
      opacity in class PotElement
      Parameters:
      opacity - the opacity value between 0.0 (transparent) and 1.0 (opaque)
      Returns:
      this element for method chaining

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class PotElement
